name: Mercy USA for Aid and Development
mission: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development is dedicated to alleviating human suffering and supporting individuals and their communities in their efforts to become more self-sufficient. Our philosophy is "Helping People Help Themselves".  Incorporated in the State of Michigan in 1988, Mercy-USA's projects focus on improving health, nutrition and access to safe water, as well as promoting economic and educational growth and providing disaster relief around the world.
theoryOfChange: Stated focus on alleviating human suffering and supporting individuals and communities in their efforts to become more self-sufficient, with projects focusing on improving health, nutrition, and access to safe water, and promoting economic and educational growth worldwide.
scoreSummary: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development, Inc. shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mercyusa.org/zakat (Source: https://mercyusa.org/zakat)

keyConcerns.headline: 30% of reported revenue is noncash (gifts-in-kind)
keyConcerns.detail: Noncash contributions inflate reported program ratios. Cash-adjusted metrics better reflect actual spending.

keyConcerns.headline: 96% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mercyusa.org/zakat (Source: https://mercyusa.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: Mercy-USA has a Charity Navigator score of 92.33333333333333/100, a program expense ratio of 96.3%, fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised, and 0.8 months of working capital.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and follows specific policies for asnaf categories.
The organization reported total revenue of $34,922,507 and a program expense ratio of 96.3%.
Mercy-USA provides nutrition support and health services for children and mothers in Somalia.
The organization builds modular homes and maintains high TB cure rates.
Mercy-USA provides safe water through wells and infrastructure projects.
The organization supports food aid in Syria and visually impaired children in Gaza.
Mercy-USA offers various donation options including Zakat.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Mercy-USA earns a strong overall rating due to its high financial efficiency and direct impact on humanitarian crises, though its low working capital reserves slightly temper its financial health score.
areas_for_improvement: Limited liquidity with only 0.8 months of working capital available [1].
Increasing financial reserves to improve long-term organizational stability.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Mercy-USA shows high alignment for Muslim donors through its Zakat-eligible status and focus on urgent humanitarian causes in the Middle East and Africa [2][9]. The organization maintains a strong track record of accountability, reflected in its Charity Navigator score of 92.33333333333333/100 [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, managing a total revenue of $34,922,507 to fund critical water infrastructure and health programs [1][3]. Its effectiveness is evidenced by high TB cure rates in Somalia and the provision of safe water to thousands of beneficiaries [6][7][8].

baseline_narrative.headline: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development provides life-saving nutrition, health services, and clean water to vulnerable populations across Somalia, Syria, and beyond.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 96.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds go directly to services [1][3].
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.03 to raise every $1 [1].
Strong track record in specialized health services, including high TB cure rates and modular home construction [6][8].
baseline_narrative.summary: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development, Inc. is a humanitarian organization dedicated to crisis relief and sustainable development, specifically focusing on maternal and child health in Somalia [3][4]. The organization is Zakat-eligible, maintaining a dedicated policy to ensure donations reach those in need according to established asnaf categories [2][9].

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently maintains only <cite id="17">0.8 months of working capital</cite>, which poses a risk to operational continuity during funding fluctuations.
While the charity tracks <cite id="18">one primary outcome metric via Candid</cite>, expanding to multi-year external evaluations would strengthen its impact evidence grade.

bbb_assessment.summary: Holds the Seal of Excellence and meets high accountability standards.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains a high program ratio, ensuring that even with reduced revenue, the majority of funds reach beneficiaries.
risk_factors: <cite id="17">Working capital of only 0.8 months</cite> creates high vulnerability to economic shifts.
<cite id="19">Negative revenue CAGR of -8.6%</cite> suggests a shrinking budget for international programs.
case_against.summary: The primary concern is financial volatility, evidenced by a <cite id="19">-8.6% three-year revenue growth rate</cite> and <cite id="17">extremely low cash reserves</cite>.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023; ratings from late 2024
confidence.notes: Strong alignment between financial filings and third-party ratings.

alignment.explanation: The charity is a <cite id="9">zakat-eligible organization</cite> serving <cite id="10">essential asnaf categories</cite> in high-urgency humanitarian zones like Gaza and Yemen.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be maximized by <cite id="21">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Mercy-USA due to its <cite id="1">38-year track record</cite>, <cite id="12">Platinum Candid status</cite>, and an <cite id="20">independent board with 100% participation</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="17">increase its operating reserves</cite> to at least 3 months to ensure long-term stability.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with <cite id="5">minimal fundraising costs</cite> and a <cite id="16">96.3% program ratio</cite> according to Charity Navigator's assessment.
impact.improvement: Impact scoring would improve by <cite id="18">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for a formal cost-per-beneficiary assessment.

rich_narrative.headline: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development provides life-saving nutrition, health services, and clean water to vulnerable populations across Somalia, Syria, and beyond.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill zakat obligations through a long-standing institution with exceptionally low overhead and a focus on maternal health in East Africa and the Middle East.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure nearly 100% of their donation reaches the field
Prioritize health and nutrition for women and children in crisis
Value established organizations with over 35 years of experience
Seek zakat-eligible giving options for humanitarian relief
giving_considerations: The organization has very low cash reserves, making it reliant on consistent monthly giving
Revenue has trended downward recently, meaning new donations are critical for maintaining program scale
Focus is on direct service (clinics, wells) rather than advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large financial endowments or those requiring rigorous RCT-level evidence of impact.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that outcomes are <cite id="18">measured and tracked via Candid</cite>, but there is no evidence of independent third-party evaluations or RCTs.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Mercy-USA utilizes a direct service model to improve health, nutrition, and water access, which in turn promotes economic and educational growth.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that health interventions like TB treatments actually lead to long-term recovery for the community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="19">revenue declining by 8.6%</cite> and <cite id="17">minimal reserves</cite>, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding to stabilize its programs.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Somalia, Syria, Yemen, Gaza, Bosnia, Kenya

peer_comparison.differentiator: Mercy-USA stands out for its <cite id="5">$0.02 fundraising efficiency</cite>, which is significantly better than the <cite id="22">industry standard of $0.25</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="5">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than industry benchmarks for humanitarian organizations.
Mercy-USA maintains a high <cite id="6">80% TB cure rate</cite> in its Somalia centers and provides critical nutrition support for <cite id="11">pregnant women and nursing mothers</cite>.
The charity holds a <cite id="12">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and achieved <cite id="13">perfect 100/100 scores in Culture and Leadership</cite> beacons.

strengths_deep_dive: Mercy-USA's operational model emphasizes direct service delivery in high-conflict zones. Their water infrastructure projects in 2023 reached <cite id="8">10,000 beneficiaries</cite>, while their seasonal food aid programs spanned <cite id="14">10 different countries</cite>. This scale is supported by a <cite id="15">96.3% program expense ratio</cite> as reported in their most recent audited filings, though Charity Navigator's cash-adjusted analysis places their <cite id="16">program efficiency at 96.3%</cite>. This discrepancy often arises from the timing of large-scale international aid shipments and domestic administrative reporting.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1988</cite>, Mercy-USA for Aid and Development is a humanitarian organization that provides critical health, nutrition, and clean water services to vulnerable populations across <cite id="2">Somalia, Syria, Yemen, and Gaza</cite>. The organization reported <cite id="3">$54,067,823 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, maintaining a <cite id="4">92.33/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>. Their operations are characterized by high efficiency, with a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.02 to raise every $1</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward frontline services. In Somalia, the charity is a key provider of maternal and child health services, notably achieving an <cite id="6">80% TB cure rate</cite> through its specialized centers. Beyond immediate medical relief, Mercy-USA invests in long-term infrastructure, having <cite id="7">built 950 modular homes</cite> for displaced families and provided <cite id="8">safe water access to 10,000 people</cite> through berkads and wells in 2023 alone. The organization is <cite id="9">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, with programs specifically designed to serve the <cite id="10">fuqara (poor), masakin (needy), and ibn sabil (wayfarers)</cite>.
